author: @richardcarew4708
text: I really enjoyed it... I have been thinking about an inside out shorter version as the exit of a pulse jet... to try to develop more thrust with a rotating lever.. a propeller... with which to move the World

replies.author: @richardcarew4708
replies.text: I will test it with an expanding screw inside the conical exhaust port, which will be gimbaled on 2 axis to see if it creates more thrust than a straight cone as used in rocket motors... I have silicon carbide as the material, lighter than aluminum and 10 on the Moh's scale of hardness.. melting point @ 3500° C... near carbon... stationary or freely rotational on diamond bearings... they are not rare.. not even a little bit... kimberlite pipes are rare.. diamond is not

author: @DeliciousDeBlair
text:
I can say that if you make it much longer, the torque developed goes up, [just gotta have a bit longer fall] and if you are using all cheap parts like this, you SHOULD be able to get up to voltages with which you can charge car batteries at least, just gotta set up the gearing commensurate to the voltage you are trying to get and you should be good to go. 

       /)/) 
    (  'ˬ') 
@(>_ )-b 
     \) \)

author: @burtybasset4486
text:
Great inventiveness!

I was thinking about rigging up a little pelton turbine with a high head near where I live with the slower moving water and since out of most of the "green" energy water is the most consistent and reliable for small scale energy generation. However it is also unfortunately the most regulated.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: That's a great idea mate and yes it is a pity

author: @davegeorge7094

author: @NorthernKitty
text: Yet another effective method of scavenging power!!  Thank you so much for showing us these projects, Rob!!!  🥰 This makes me even more determined to find a vacant piece of land with a stream on it for homesteading.  I imagine a wide variety of devices like this and various windmills all with unique shapes and decorative designs that make them more like living sculptures while they churn out power.  😊  To make the basin, I was thinking perhaps making a mold that you could fill with something cheap, durable and light that would harden so you could quickly churn them out as needed.  Perhaps the screw as well, but all that comes to mind for that is epoxy resin, and that would be a bit expensive.  Afraid my brain is just too foggy tonight to think of what substances might work for quick/cheap molding.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I like the homestead idea if you get round to it can. send you a few devices for test? - if it was me I would build a vacuum former and use HDPE tbh

replies.author: @NorthernKitty
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering If I can ever make that dream a reality, it would absolutely be my pleasure to try some of these projects!!  😊  I'm actually getting much closer to the first step in that goal, which is converting something mobile (shuttle bus, cargo trailer) into livable space, ready to plop down on a vacant lot when I find one.  Re: HDPE and vacuum forming, that never occurred to me as a DIY option... are you hinting at a future series, perhaps?  😁

author: @Laser2120
text: We have so much water and streams in the UK we could do with making use of the free energy they could provide. But guarantee people would complain becuase they don't like the look of them or they churn up a few frogs. I know we do use the archimedes screw to generate on some private rivers but its not as common as it should be. P.s Was that a frog being churned up or a leaf ?

author: @WileHeCoyote
text: That is awesome ROB! Has to be at least a few watts your getting right!? I believe you mentioned in a previous video that  the "secret sauce" in making usable or practical power from a stream sized flow rate is in the motor to screw ratio and screw pitch to flow rate/volume ratios? is there a simple system to make it easily adjustable at both ends? ive seen simple cheap 3 speed RC car transmissions that may make it more adaptable for changing flow rates of a stream.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I suppose so mate - the reason I don't. look into that yet is this is really just a promising design and there is certainly work to do on it - was we know is the principle works now we need to look at combining a 'good' schema for generation and we haven't done that yet

author: @pulesjet
text: Any means to produced a useful voltage is worth knowing about.  If everyone just made that little effort there would be no power issues.   1500 square feet of my side yard all but powers our home.  Between Solar and Wind my electric bills have gone from $150.00 a month to $3o.00..  Only reason I'm stuck with that is I was stupid and used a 240vac pump in my 200ft deep well.  When that dies the problems will be solved.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice one mate and I agree
